ks1.objective: Avoid the taggers. If tagged, call your team for a selfie rescue with the orange cone.
successCriteria: Moves around the area without bumping into others

lks2.objective: Combine dodging, rescuing teammates and protecting the 'mobile phone' cone.
successCriteria: Scans the area for taggers and rescue chances

uks2.objective: Apply scanning and decision-making with footballs introduced — tag, rescue and dribble simultaneously.
successCriteria: Targets the toughest taggers/runners as a top performer

space.description: A square area suitable for the age and number of children. Children start without a football but have one ready to introduce later.

value.name: Friendship
value.text: At the Football Fun Factory, smiles and kindness are part of everything we do. World Smile Day is on the 4th of October — a whole day dedicated to smiles and acts of kindness. Today we're bringing out even more smiles.

steps: Start with two taggers — they try to tag as many players as possible within 60 seconds.
The rest of the group must avoid being tagged.
If a player is tagged, they stand with their hands on their hips and call their teammates for help.
A tagged player can be 'freed' if a teammate takes a selfie with them using one of the orange cones.
If a player holding a 'mobile phone' (cone) is tagged, they drop the phone and a teammate can pick it up.
progressions: Rotate roles and change the taggers regularly.
Change the number of mobile phones.
Introduce footballs for both the taggers and the runners.
questions: What's the best place to stand if you want to rescue someone?
How do you decide whether to rescue or run?
Where do the 'mobile phones' need to be to stay safe?

step.space: Smaller area = tougher challenge for the runners
step.task: Top performers specifically go after other top performers
step.equipment: Vary number of mobile phones / taggers
step.people: Add footballs for both taggers and runners

base.showmanship: Talk about World Smile Day in front of the parents and ask the children if they can do a random act of kindness to make someone smile.
